items.title: Bonus Hollander POTD Bubbles Hawkins. Illinois St SG drafted 51st by GS in 75. Barely played & cut. Signed by NJ in 76-77 & had 19.3 ppg but fought w/coach & was cut in 77-78. NBA career was over after a 4 gm stint w/DET in 78-79. Struggled after & was sadly killed in 93. RIP.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst
items.body_excerpt: Bonus Hollander POTD Bubbles Hawkins. Illinois St SG drafted 51st by GS in 75. Barely played & cut. Signed by NJ in 76-77 & had 19.3 ppg but fought w/coach & was cut in 77-78. NBA career was over after a 4 gm stint w/DET in 78-79. Struggled after & was sadly killed in 93. RIP.

items.title: Tyler Marsh on the Sky's pick-and-roll defense:
items.author: Drew
items.body_excerpt:
Tyler Marsh on the Sky's pick-and-roll defense:

"We have kind of the mantra of 'nobody on an island'... We can't just expect Kamilla to be involved in ball screens and then leave her out there by herself."

Ball pressure and help defense are part of the equation, he said.

items.title: This is why I refuse to use the 'insider' tag. You're either a reporter, an entertainer, an analyst, or a combination of these hats. And if you're a reporter, you are by definition an insider, or you're not doing your job. You aren't special for doing it. Your work might be.

items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
I also asked Dillon Mitchell about how his time at St. John’s helped him grow as a passer.

“I think it came down to that we didn't really have like a true point guard last year. So, in practice, he kind of seen me rebound the ball, push it, try to make plays.

“I'm a very selfless person. I…” 1/x

items.title: As always, I'd recommend against assuming that a team's roster is completely finished on the afternoon of June 30th.
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
As always, I'd recommend against assuming that a team's roster is completely finished on the afternoon of June 30th.

Could the Hawks be done now? Theoretically!

Are they also allowed to still make trades and free agent signings with room to maneuver below the tax line? Yep!

items.author: Spencer Davies

items.title: LeBron James played with the Lakers for eight seasons, his longest single stint with a franchise. He averaged 25.9 points, 7.7 rebounds, and 7.9 assists per game. His accolades in LA include a title, eight All-Star appearances, and making seven All-NBA teams. What a run.
